description_en: Johannes Chrysostomus Wolfgangus Theophilus Mozart born January 27 1756 in Getreidegasse 9 Salzburg. Seventh child of Leopold Mozart and Anna Maria Pertl; only he and sister Nannerl survive infancy
At three years old mimics his sister Nannnerls keyboard lessons immediately grasping chords tonality and tempo. Father Leopold begins tutoring him
Composes his first pieces — Minuet and Trio K.1 and K.2 — at age five. Leopold carefully notates them beginning a catalog that will reach over 800 works
Performs before the court in Munich and Vienna. Famously climbs onto Empress Maria Theresas lap at Schonbrunn Palace. His career as a prodigy officially begins
Leopold sets off with the family on a three-year grand tour — Munich Augsburg Paris London Amsterdam. Wolfgang performs and composes at every stop

event_short: Meets Johann Christian Bach in London
description_en: During the London stay Mozart meets Johann Christian Bach — a formative influence on his melodic style and approach to keyboard concertos
Composes his first symphonies during the European tour along with 16 sonatas for keyboard and violin — showcasing astonishing command of large musical forms
Composes his first opera Apollo et Hyacinthus K.38 at age eleven for a university performance in Salzburg
Emperor Joseph II commissions La finta semplice — Viennese opera intrigue delays its premiere a year giving the twelve-year-old his first lesson in court politics
Leopold takes Wolfgang to Italy — the musical capital of Europe. The tour is a triumph of performances and commissions
Hears the Vaticans jealously guarded Miserere by Allegri once in the Sistine Chapel then writes out the entire score from memory — causing a sensation across Europe

event_short: Knight of the Golden Spur by Pope
description_en: Pope Clement XIV awards Mozart the Order of the Golden Spur in Rome — an honor previously given to Gluck
The Accademia Filarmonica of Bologna accepts Mozart despite a rule requiring candidates to be at least twenty. He passes their composition exam in under an hour
Appointed Konzertmeister — leader of the court orchestra — by the Archbishop of Salzburg. Paid but constrained; the beginning of a restless decade
Composes all five violin concertos during his Salzburg years along with the Sinfonia Concertante and various masses
Given leave by the Archbishop sets off with his mother to Mannheim Paris and Munich seeking employment and creative freedom
In Mannheim becomes deeply infatuated with sixteen-year-old soprano Aloysia Weber. Leopold writes alarmed letters urging his son to stay focused on his career
Anna Maria Mozart falls ill and dies in Paris on July 3 1778. Mozart is alone in a foreign city grieving and unable to find work

event_short: Returns to Salzburg as court organist
description_en: Returns to Salzburg depressed and unsuccessful but his father secures him a position as court organist — composing masses symphonies and piano sonatas
Breaks with Archbishop Colloredo — reportedly kicked out by the court steward — and stays in Vienna as a freelance musician. A bold reckless gamble
Now lodging with the Weber family — Aloysia having married elsewhere — falls in love with younger sister Constanze. Leopold disapproves
His first mature opera Idomeneo K.366 premieres to great success in Munich — a breakthrough in operatic form and dramatic expression
Marries Constanze Weber on August 4 against his fathers wishes. Of their six children only Karl Thomas and Franz Xaver Wolfgang survive infancy
Composes the Mass in C minor K.427 — not for commission but as a personal vow connected to his marriage. One of his most ambitious sacred works
Initiated into the Freemason lodge Zur Wohlthatigkeit in Vienna — an influential intellectual circle. Masonic ideals will shape his later work especially The Magic Flute
Publishes six string quartets dedicated to Joseph Haydn who tells Leopold: Your son is the greatest composer I know. A landmark in chamber music
Le nozze di Figaro K.492 premieres in Vienna on May 1. Subversive comic and emotionally truthful — one of the most performed operas in history
Leopold Mozart dies May 28 in Salzburg. The complicated devoted controlling relationship that defined Wolfgangs entire life comes to an end
Don Giovanni K.527 premieres in Prague to extraordinary acclaim on October 29. Mozart reportedly says: For my Praguers!
Emperor Joseph II appoints Mozart as Imperial Chamber Composer in Vienna — a prestigious sinecure replacing the late Gluck

event_short: Last three symphonies in six weeks
description_en: Composes symphonies No.39 in E-flat No.40 in G minor and No.41 Jupiter in C major — within six weeks. A remarkable creative burst
Travels to Dresden Leipzig and Berlin. King Frederick William II reportedly offers Kapellmeister position — Mozart declines out of loyalty to Vienna
Sends desperate letters to fellow Freemason Michael Puchberg begging for loans. The family is in serious debt — concert income has dried up

event_short: Appointed assistant Kapellmeister at St. Stephens
description_en: Appointed unpaid assistant Kapellmeister at St. Stephens Cathedral — contracted to succeed to the paid role; financial hope at last
Completes Die Zauberflote K.620 for the Freihaustheater. Premieres September 30 to great public success. Mozart conducts the first performances
An anonymous messenger arrives with a commission for a Requiem — sent by Count von Walsegg who intends to pass it off as his own. Mozart grows obsessed with finishing it

event_short: La Clemenza di Tito premieres in Prague
description_en: La Clemenza di Tito K.621 premieres in Prague on September 6 for the coronation of Leopold II as King of Bohemia — composed in just eighteen days
In November Mozarts health collapses rapidly. Bedridden he works on the Requiem until his final days convinced he is writing his own funeral mass
Dies in Vienna December 5 1791 aged 35. Cause recorded as severe miliary fever — modern scholars debate whether it was rheumatic fever kidney disease or other illness. The Requiem remains unfinished
